rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness with immaculate facilities and amenities that are consistently described as spotless and pristine, maintained daily by attentive hosts Beautiful riverside location on the Severn River with abundant birdlife, scenic views, and close proximity to Girraween National Park (20 minutes) and Granite Belt wineries Outstanding hosts Ben and Narelle who receive extensive praise for friendliness and helpfulness, creating a welcoming atmosphere with strong repeat visitation
rationale_honourable: Country Style Holiday Park earns Silver recognition for its exceptional cleanliness, beautiful riverside location, and outstanding hosts Ben and Narelle who consistently go above and beyond for guests. Families appreciate the spacious sites, well-maintained facilities, pet-friendly atmosphere with off-leash dog park, and proximity to Granite Belt wineries and Girraween National Park, making it an excellent base for exploring the Stanthorpe region.
water_fun: River swimming and kayaking (seasonal water levels)
kids_play: Goats and sheep to feed, giant chess
pet_detail: Pet friendly with dedicated off-leash dog park featuring agility equipment; pets welcome in cabins and throughout park on lead
best_for: Families seeking a peaceful, pet-friendly riverside retreat with farm animals, spotless facilities, and welcoming hosts who create a relaxed country atmosphere near wine country and national parks
executive_summary:
PARK: Country Style Holiday Park
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 351

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (11/20) — Limited dedicated kids entertainment with goats and sheep to feed, giant chess board, and communal fire pit being the main activities; no playground, jumping pillow, or waterpark facilities mentioned; families noted kids enjoyed feeding animals and playing in nature but infrastructure for children's entertainment is minimal.

Nature (17/20) — Beautiful riverside location on the Severn River with abundant birdlife, scenic water views, and access to swimming/kayaking; close proximity to Girraween National Park (20 minutes) and surrounded by Granite Belt wineries; river sometimes low or dry during drought but peaceful bush setting with wildlife; families loved the natural environment and tranquil atmosphere.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Consistently described as "immaculate", "spotless", and "pristine" with amenities and grounds receiving exceptional praise; camp kitchen well-equipped and clean; Ben and Narelle clearly take pride in maintenance with daily attention to facilities; rare minor complaints about brown tank water quality and occasional septic smell.

Value (12/15) — Generally considered good value with reasonable rates (powered sites $41-50, showers $1 for 6 minutes); families appreciated spacious sites and clean facilities for the price; some felt shower charges were steep given nightly rates; overall positive value perception though not exceptional compared to amenities offered.

Site Size (9/10) — Large, spacious drive-through powered sites consistently praised; unpowered riverside sites described as roomy with good spacing between campers; suitable for big rigs and motorhomes; occasional mention of tight turning spaces and some sites lacking grass, but overwhelmingly positive feedback on site sizes.

Sentiment (9/10) — Very high satisfaction with hosts Ben and Narelle receiving extensive praise for friendliness and helpfulness; strong repeat visitation and recommendations; some complaints about management attitude toward families with children and handling of minor issues; majority of families felt welcomed and plan to return.

Location (10/10) — Excellent strategic location 5-10 minutes south of Stanthorpe, close to numerous wineries (one directly across road), 20 minutes to Girraween National Park, and convenient to Granite Belt attractions; easy highway access while maintaining peaceful country feel; families valued central positioning for exploring region.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Minimal on-site entertainment infrastructure for children beyond feeding animals and giant chess, with no playground, waterpark, or structured kids activities that families with young children typically seek in holiday parks.
2. River water levels highly variable and sometimes completely dry during drought periods, disappointing families who booked specifically for water activities; some guests felt inadequately informed about water conditions prior to arrival.
3. Management style inconsistencies with several families reporting dismissive or confrontational responses to concerns, particularly regarding families with children, and issues with handling camp rules around fires and site allocation creating negative experiences for some guests.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Immaculate facilities and park", "spotless amenities", "Ben and Narelle were fantastic", "great location right opposite Winery", "goats and sheep are friendly", "off leash dog area was awesome", "river was low due to drought", "too many rules everywhere"

park_name: Top of the Town Tourist Park
rationale_top3: Nature (16/20): Strong natural bushland setting with terraced camping among granite rocks, described as 'mini Girraween on the edge of town' with stunning views and easy access to national park attractions Cleanliness (12/15): Modern amenity blocks generally kept spotless with positive feedback, though cabin cleanliness shows significant inconsistency during peak periods Value (12/15): Reasonable pricing for powered sites with modern facilities, though cabin value questioned by families and pricing inconsistencies noted across similar site types
rationale_honourable: Top of the Town Tourist Park earns Silver status for its consistently praised natural bushland setting and clean modern amenities, with families particularly appreciating the peaceful atmosphere and proximity to Girraween National Park. While it offers a pool and camp kitchens that families value, the park shows its age with maintenance issues and lacks dedicated kids' entertainment facilities, making it better suited as a base for exploring the region rather than a destination with on-site activities for young children.
water_fun: Small pool available, teens sometimes unruly
kids_play: No dedicated playground, trip to town needed
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with room for walks
best_for: Families with older children who want a peaceful bushland setting for nature-based exploration rather than structured kids' activities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Top of the Town Tourist Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 107

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (10/20) — Limited on-site entertainment with one reviewer noting "nothing much to amuse the kids" requiring "a quick trip into town to the playground." The pool receives mixed feedback with families reporting "kids loved the pool" but several noting teenagers jumping from rocks made it unsafe, with "families with young children had to get out." One review mentioned "surprisingly good facilities for the kids" but most family reviews focus on using the park as a base rather than destination entertainment.

Nature (16/20) — Strong natural environment with multiple reviews praising the bushland setting. Families noted "beautiful bush up the back of the park," "like a mini Girraween on the edge of town," "stunning views," and "felt like camping in the bush." The park backs onto a hill with terraced camping areas among granite rocks. Easy access to Girraween National Park mentioned by families. One reviewer with kids specifically praised "lovely park, lots to explore, our kids had a great time" in the natural setting.

Cleanliness (12/15) — Generally positive with most reviews praising "very clean amenities," "spotless amenities," and modern facilities. However, consistency is an issue with several families reporting problems: "amenities could of been better cleaned," "shower block could do with more attention on weekends," and two scathing 1-star reviews describing cabins as "extremely dirty," "filthy bathroom," and "mould in the shower." Recent reviews more positive about amenity blocks being kept clean during busy periods.

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback on value. Positive reviews note "prices are very reasonable," "good value country camp ground," and "reasonable price with modern amenities." However, families staying in cabins reported concerns: "pretty hefty price for what we got," "barely had the bare essentials," and confusion over inconsistent pricing with some paying $27-42 for similar sites. One family felt they "paid $20 to just not have to stay another night." The customer service issues around refunds for medical emergencies damaged value perception.

Site Size (7/10) — Site sizes vary considerably. Positive mentions include "good sized sites," "nice roomie sites," and "big sites, some drive through." However, families with caravans noted challenges: "some are rather small, 55-60 m2," "quite a few with no slabs," "extremely hard to navigate and uneven," "quite unlevel," and "difficult to level the caravan on some sites." The terraced layout on the hillside creates access challenges. Ensuite sites described as "large" with good spacing.

Sentiment (7/10) — Sentiment is inconsistent across family reviews. Strong positive reviews state "absolutely loved it and cant wait to return" and "kids had a great time." However, several families had negative experiences, particularly those in cabins during peak periods. One family with young children placed near a Christmas party until 10:15pm and woken at 4:50am called it "vile" and "derelict." Another family cut their holiday short. The rigid no-refund policy during medical emergencies upset families. Pool incidents with unsupervised teenagers also frustrated families with young children.

Location (9/10) — Location consistently praised as "close to town," "5 minute easy walk," "15 min walk to main hub," and "great base to visit attractions of Stanthorpe." Families appreciated proximity to shops, cafes, restaurants, and "easy drive to Girraween National Park." Located beside showgrounds with markets next door. Access to wineries, truffle farms, and other attractions convenient. Only minor criticism: "a little far out of the town centre" for walking with small children.

WHY NOT 100:
The park lacks dedicated kids' entertainment and playground facilities, requiring families to drive into town for children's activities, significantly limiting its appeal for families with young children seeking on-site entertainment. Site quality is inconsistent with multiple families reporting unlevel pitches, poor road conditions, and difficulty accessing sites, particularly problematic for families with larger rigs and young children. Cleanliness and maintenance show significant variability, especially in cabins which received several scathing reviews from families describing filthy conditions, with "mould in shower," "ants in beds," and outdated facilities that don't meet family standards, while customer service issues around inflexible refund policies and poor handling of noise complaints from families with young children damaged the family-friendly reputation.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids loved the pool", "our kids had a great time", "nothing much to amuse the kids", "families with young children had to get out", "mini Girraween on the edge of town", "young kids awake until 10:15pm", "roads are in very bad condition", "extremely dirty", "showing its age", "beautiful bush up the back"
